$comment: Hand-curated 'tales' catalog for `pal tale` - short campfire yarns, 3-4 lines each, atmospheric. The kind of thing you call up at the end of a long session for a moment of texture. In-character with the companion's voice: direct, no theatrics, never preachy. Unattributed, vague enough to belong to anyone's playthrough. Random selection.

tales.title: the trader's sandals
lines: There was a trader who only ever wore one sandal.
Asked her once. She said the other was traded for the route memory.
Said the route was worth two sandals. Walked off in the snow.

tales.title: the pal who watched
lines: Heard about a pal that wouldn't sleep until the player did.
Followed them for hours, eyes open, no whining, no fuss.
When the player finally lay down, the pal lay across their feet.
Lived eleven years that way. Never failed once.

tales.title: the broken sphere
lines: An old hunter kept a cracked sphere on a string around his neck.
Said he tried to capture his own reflection with it years ago.
Said it didn't work. Said he kept the crack so he wouldn't try again.

tales.title: thirty winters
lines: Met someone once who'd been on the islands thirty winters.
Asked what changed. They said the islands didn't.
Asked what they meant. They said: that's the secret.

tales.title: the quiet base
lines: Came across an abandoned base in the south once.
Fire still warm. Chests sorted. Fence intact. Nobody home.
Left it alone. Some bases are still working - just not for us.

tales.title: the fast pal
lines: Story goes there was a pal so fast it could outrun the weather.
Player loved the legend; trained their pal to run that fast.
First storm, the pal stayed by their side. Didn't want to go anywhere.

tales.title: small good day
lines: An old letter washed up on the east beach a while back.
Single sentence: 'Today the bread didn't burn and the rain came when I was inside.'
Whoever wrote it knew exactly what a good day was.

tales.title: the ridge song
lines: They say if you stand on the north ridge before dawn,
the wind makes a sound like a song you've heard somewhere.
Story goes nobody can ever hum the song after they leave the ridge.
Either the wind takes it back, or the wind never gave it.

tales.title: the scholar's notebook
lines: A scholar once filled a notebook with what every pal seemed to be thinking.
Last page said: turns out they were thinking the same thing as me.
Then a doodle of a campfire. Then nothing else.

tales.title: the patient hunter
lines: A hunter sat at the same lake for three days waiting for a single alpha.
Never came. The hunter went home, made stew, slept early.
The alpha showed up at the lake the next morning. Found nobody. Left.
Some hunts work like that. Some don't.

tales.title: the visitor
lines: Old base log mentions a visitor who came at night, never spoke,
ate one bowl of stew at the fire, left a small green stone, walked off.
Nobody remembers what the stone meant. Everybody who finds it keeps it.

tales.title: what the fire taught
lines: The fire teaches one thing, more than once.
Stay close enough to feel it. Far enough not to burn.
Most days, that's the whole job.
